categories.name: QUERY_INFO
categories.precondition: Target data-bearing screen reachable from launcher; target item/query specifies a filter (date, status, priority, attribute, etc.); app may not be in foreground
abstract_steps: Launch the relevant app from the launcher
Navigate to the primary list/overview screen or to the named item by browsing the list or using an in-app lookup affordance
Resolve any relative terms in the query (today, next week, after next) to absolute values using the device/task context
Scroll through the full list to enumerate all candidate items
Inspect the visible marker/attribute (icon, prefix, checkbox, color) that indicates the attribute in question
Apply the query's filter predicate to each item using observable visual attributes
Aggregate matching items into the requested output format (list, count, yes/no, categorical value)
Emit a single answer action, then immediately emit a terminal completion signal
failure_modes: Declaring the task infeasible from the launcher without attempting to open the app
Declaring the query infeasible before exhausting available navigation (scroll, open detail)
Answering based on a screen that does not actually contain the target item
Answering from a partial view without scrolling the full list
Looping on the same answer action without finalizing with a completion status
Repeating the same answer action without terminating, causing step exhaustion
Confusing relative date labels with the queried absolute date
Guessing semantic attributes (priority, category) from unrelated visual cues
Miscounting due to duplicated or partially visible rows after scrolling
Modifying data while reading
Reporting stale/cached values
verification_checklist: The target item's row is actually visible on screen
The attribute marker is unambiguously present or absent
Did I reach the list screen that actually contains the data?
Did I map all relative terms to concrete values consistent with the current date?
Did I scroll until the end of the list (no new content appears)?
Does each returned item satisfy every part of the filter (date AND status AND priority)?
Is the answer format exactly what the user requested (comma-separated / count / None)?
A single completion/status signal is emitted after the answer
Did I emit a terminal completion action exactly once after answering?
Reported value matches on-screen value
No side effects on data

categories.name: COUNT_ITEMS_BY_ATTRIBUTE
categories.precondition: A container (folder/list/category) and a filtering attribute are specified
abstract_steps: Open the app and navigate to the specified container via its navigation drawer/menu
Enumerate all list entries, scrolling as needed to cover the full list
Count entries whose row carries the filtering attribute marker
Return the count as a number
failure_modes: Counting within the wrong container because the app opened to a different default
Missing entries that require scrolling
Confusing unrelated visual markers with the target attribute
verification_checklist: Current container label matches the requested one
List has been scrolled to end or confirmed fully visible
Only entries with the specific attribute marker are counted

categories.name: FILTER_BY_ATTRIBUTE
categories.precondition: List view exposes per-item visual attributes (color, icon, label) that encode the attribute of interest
abstract_steps: Identify the concrete visual encoding of the target attribute on this app (e.g., checkbox color = priority)
Iterate items and apply the encoding rule
Return matching titles/identifiers
failure_modes: Assuming a default encoding from other apps rather than observing this app's cues
Treating urgency/date as priority when priority is encoded separately
verification_checklist: Is the attribute encoding confirmed by at least one unambiguous example?
Are negative examples (items that should NOT match) excluded?

categories.name: LOOKUP_FIELD_IN_ENTRY
categories.precondition: Entry title and desired field/sub-item are specified
abstract_steps: Open the app and invoke its entry-lookup affordance
Provide the entry title and select the matching result
Locate the section containing the field and read its value
Return the value verbatim
failure_modes: Skipping app launch and declaring infeasible
Opening a wrong result with a similar title
Reading from the wrong section of the entry
verification_checklist: Opened entry title matches the requested one
Value is read from the correct labeled section
Answer preserves units/qualifiers as shown

categories.name: NAVIGATE_TO
categories.precondition: Multiple containers exist or target app/screen may not be on current screen; a specific one is required
abstract_steps: If target app not visible, invoke the app-launch primitive or open the system launcher/app list to switch context
Open the navigation drawer/menu
Locate the named container (scroll if necessary)
Traverse the container hierarchy via click-in and back-navigation
Tap to switch context
Verify the landing screen/breadcrumb/title matches expectations before acting
failure_modes: Assuming the currently opened container is the target
Missing the target entry because it resides in a different container
Confusing similar container identifiers
Getting stuck in side menus (e.g., configuration panes) that do not lead to target
Repeating the same failing click
Acting before the landing screen is fully loaded
verification_checklist: Header/title of the list reflects the requested container name
Breadcrumb/title matches expected path
Expected content visible
Title bar/landmark of destination screen is visible

categories.name: ADD_ENTRY
categories.precondition: App is open at a list/index view that exposes an affordance to create a new item
abstract_steps: Invoke the primary create-new affordance (commonly a prominent action button)
Provide the required identifier in the creation dialog, clearing any prefilled placeholder first
Select the correct item kind/format if the dialog offers a choice
Confirm creation
If opened in an editor, enter the required body content
Persist the change (explicit commit if auto-persistence is not guaranteed)
failure_modes: Leaving the prefilled placeholder identifier concatenated with the intended identifier
Not switching item kind (leaf vs container) before confirming
Garbled extension/suffix field producing wrong format
Declaring success before persisting
Creating item in wrong parent container
verification_checklist: New item appears in the listing with the exact expected identifier
Opening the item shows the expected body content
No stray/extra items created
Correct parent container

categories.name: REMOVE_ENTRY
categories.precondition: Target item is visible in a list view
abstract_steps: Long-press the target row to enter selection mode
Invoke the removal action from the contextual action bar
Confirm removal in the confirmation dialog
failure_modes: Selecting wrong row (similar identifiers)
Missing the confirmation step
Removing parent container instead of the target item
verification_checklist: Item no longer appears in the listing
No confirmation dialog left open
Sibling items intact

categories.name: BULK_REMOVE
categories.precondition: Multiple items to clear from a list
abstract_steps: Repeat the single-item removal flow for each item, OR use multi-select if available
After each removal, verify return to list before selecting the next target
failure_modes: Index shifting after removal leading to wrong next item
Stopping before all targets are cleared
verification_checklist: Listing contains only non-target items (often empty)

categories.name: EDIT_ENTRY_CONTENT
categories.precondition: Target item exists and can be opened in an editor
abstract_steps: Open the item from the list
Position cursor at the required location (start, end, or full-content selection)
Insert, append, prepend, or replace text as required
Persist the change
failure_modes: Appending instead of prepending (or vice versa)
Not selecting the full existing content before replacing (causing concatenation)
Forgetting trailing newline when prepending
Not persisting before navigating away
verification_checklist: Item body matches expected content exactly
Line breaks and ordering match specification
Changes persisted (indicator dismisses/returns to list cleanly)

categories.name: RELABEL_ENTRY
categories.precondition: Item exists in a list view that supports relabeling
abstract_steps: Long-press the item to enter selection mode
Choose the relabel action from the contextual action bar
Enter the new identifier in the dialog and confirm
failure_modes: Attempting to relabel from within the editor (title click does not relabel)
Typing into the editor title field and assuming it relabeled the item
Leaving old extension/suffix when a new one is required
verification_checklist: List shows the item with the new identifier
Old identifier no longer present
Content preserved

categories.name: RELOCATE_ENTRY
categories.precondition: Source item and destination container both exist
abstract_steps: Long-press the source item to select it
Open the contextual overflow and choose the relocate action
In the destination picker, navigate up and/or into the target container
Confirm with the 'choose this container' action
failure_modes: Selecting the source's own container as destination
Stopping at the picker without a final confirmation
Not navigating up to the common ancestor first
verification_checklist: Item no longer present in source container
Item present in destination container
Content unchanged

categories.name: CROSS_APP_TRANSFER
categories.precondition: Source content is prepared; destination app is installed
abstract_steps: Create/finalize source content in the origin app and persist it
Launch the destination app via the launcher
Initiate a new message/record in the destination app
Provide the recipient/target identifier
Insert the content (enter it directly or transfer via system transfer buffer)
Send/commit
failure_modes: Opening the wrong destination app (a similar but different communication app)
Not persisting source before switching apps
Leaving the draft unsent
Using a pre-existing contact instead of the required recipient identifier
verification_checklist: Destination app shows the sent/committed item with exact content
Recipient identifier matches
No residual draft

categories.name: MERGE_ENTRIES
categories.precondition: Multiple source items and a specified destination identifier
abstract_steps: Create the destination item first (empty)
For each source in order: open, select full content, capture it, return to destination, insert it, append a separator if required
Persist destination
failure_modes: Getting stuck trying to create destination from editor menus instead of the list view
Losing captured content between app switches
Wrong order
Missing separators between sections
verification_checklist: Destination contains each source's content in the required order
Separators (newlines) between sections
Source items unchanged

categories.name: TRANSCRIBE_FROM_MEDIA
categories.precondition: Media source and transcription target available
abstract_steps: Open the media player and start playback
Observe each piece of text/frame
Switch to the transcription editor and record the observation with the required formatting
Repeat until media ends
Persist the transcription
failure_modes: Using 'wait' without switching back to the media app
Losing place when switching apps repeatedly
Fabricating content not actually shown
Incorrect delimiter formatting
verification_checklist: Transcription contains the complete ordered sequence
Formatting (delimiter, identifier) matches spec
Transcription persisted
